description: The worker-launch isolation fix is tracked as its own harness task so future sessions can resume from the tmux/bootstrap failure evidence directly.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only tracks the worker-launch isolation harness.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry records the tmux zsh bootstrap error, the host-shell Codex run, and the global Codex config mismatch.
Confirm the scope is limited to worker startup isolation, launch observability, and real Codex validation.

description: CodexWorkerLauncher uses an isolated shell session instead of tmux's default interactive zsh environment.
steps: Inspect the tmux launcher code and verify new sessions no longer boot into the default zsh login shell.
Verify reused sessions still relaunch work through the same isolated shell path.
Confirm the launcher keeps the existing tmux reuse and interrupt semantics.

description: The worker launch command explicitly overrides unstable global Codex config so background runs do not inherit incompatible reasoning-summary settings.
steps: Inspect the generated Codex command line for a coding task launch.
Verify it includes a compatible reasoning-summary override and still runs from the target repo root.
Confirm the user-level ~/.codex/config.toml file is not modified.

description: Worker logs and status views surface meaningful startup failures instead of collapsing to shell prompt noise.
steps: Inspect a pane/log stream with startup errors and verify the most useful error line is preserved instead of a bare shell prompt.
Verify stderr is captured into the worker artifact log alongside Codex JSON output.
Confirm coding-task status remains readable for successful launches.

description: Focused tests cover isolated shell startup, Codex config overrides, prompt/log behavior, and progress extraction regressions.
steps: Run focused pytest coverage for worker, routing, harness, reporting, and progress modules.
Verify the suite covers both the new shell wrapper and startup-error summarization paths.
Confirm compileall still succeeds for all touched modules and tests.

description: A real codex-remote launch path shows the worker enters true Codex execution after choosing 按新任务开始, without the previous brew/zsh bootstrap failure.
steps: Launch a real coding task against /Users/miau/Documents/codex-remote after the completed-harness confirmation step.
Verify the tmux pane no longer shows the brew shell bootstrap error or the earlier operation-not-permitted failure.
Confirm the artifact log and pane include real codex exec --json events.

description: README and PROGRESS document the isolated worker-launch behavior, validation evidence, and any remaining runtime caveats.
steps: Open README.md and verify the coding-task section explains the isolated worker environment at a high level.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify it records the launch failure root cause, the new launch strategy, and the final validation result.
Confirm PLAN.json is fully marked complete once all verification passes.
